data.title: Ripple introduces Mint to boost institutional access to RLUSD as stablecoin’s market cap nears $1.6B.
data.description: In a recent development, Ripple has introduced Mint to enhance access to RLUSD for institutions. The stablecoin, RLUSD, is on the brink of reaching a market cap of $1.6 billion. This move by Ripple aims to facilitate greater usage of RLUSD among institutional investors.

data.title: Poolin, a Bitcoin mining pool, declares Chapter 11 bankruptcy and plans to sell $52 million worth of its Texas mining sites.
data.description: Poolin, a Bitcoin mining company, has sought Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection and has started the process of selling its two mining sites in West Texas for $52 million as part of a plan to recover funds for creditors in the future.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Real Estate Investing Announces Acquisition of French Logistics Portfolio of Five Assets
data.description: In Paris, Morgan Stanley Investment Management, led by investment funds managed by Morgan Stanley Real Estate Investing (MSREI), has acquired a portfolio comprising five French logistics assets. This fully leased portfolio spans roughly 160,000 square meters in key French logistics hubs such as Paris, Lille, Bordeaux, Nîmes, and Tours. MSREI aims to employ an active asset management approach to boost long-term value and support the growth of these assets.

data.title: Thales to Strengthen Romania’s Airspace Protection With Twelve Ground Master 200 MM/A Radars
data.description: In Meudon, France, Thales has announced plans to enhance Romania's airspace defense by providing twelve Ground Master 200 MM/A radars. This initiative aims to bolster the country's security measures and ensure comprehensive protection in the airspace.

data.title: EU expands ownership ban to all crypto service providers for Belarusians, starting Aug. 25.
data.description: Starting on August 25, the European Union will prohibit individuals who are nationals or residents of Belarus from owning, controlling, or managing crypto firms that are regulated under the MiCA framework. This measure aims to restrict Belarusian involvement in these specific financial entities within the EU.
